description_long: This helicopter features a sleek, modern design with a streamlined gray fuselage accented by black and yellow details. The main rotor consists of four long, slender blades with yellow tips, and the tail boom extends to a fenestron-style enclosed tail rotor. The cockpit is equipped with large, angular windows providing wide visibility, and the side doors are outlined in black with visible window panels. The landing gear consists of sturdy black skids, supporting the helicopter for stable ground contact. The overall appearance suggests a utility or light transport helicopter suitable for urban operations, with a robust yet aerodynamic build.
description: A modern gray helicopter with black and yellow accents, featuring large cockpit windows, four-blade main rotor, and sturdy landing skids.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pointed nose, wide cockpit windows, and the symmetrical spread of the four main rotor blades.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the cockpit, side doors, landing skids, and the extended tail boom with the enclosed tail rotor.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail boom, fenestron-style tail rotor, and the alignment of the main rotor blades.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing cockpit windows, side doors, landing skids, and the tail structure.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four long, slender main rotor blades, likely made of composite material, with gray surfaces and yellow tips. The blades are evenly spaced and appear to be of equal length, extending well beyond the fuselage.
attribute_tail_rotor: Enclosed fenestron-style tail rotor with multiple short blades arranged within a circular housing at the end of the tail boom. The housing is black, providing protection and noise reduction.
attribute_landing_skids: Two parallel black landing skids made of metal or composite material, attached to the fuselage with angled supports. The skids are straight and robust, designed for stable ground contact.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Large, angular cockpit windows with a wraparound design, including two main front panels and additional side panels. The windows are tinted and framed in black, providing wide visibility for pilots.
